skill.name: mcp-integration
skill.description: Model Context Protocol integration guidance for designing MCP server configurations, service connectors, authentication patterns, performance improvements, and troubleshooting workflows.
skill.summary: Design, configure, and review MCP integrations for developer tools and external services.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, weak cryptography, and a critical combined-risk heuristic. Review found no executable script, prompt injection, real secret, credential exfiltration, or weak cryptographic implementation in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is medium because the skill recommends MCP configurations that run unpinned npm packages and handle API token placeholders.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Package Execution in MCP Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill shows MCP configurations that execute npm packages with npx and latest-version package references. This is legitimate MCP guidance, but it can expose users to supply-chain risk if package names, versions, or publishers are not verified before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are directly present in configuration examples, not executable skill code. The risk depends on user adoption of generated configurations without package pinning or review.

medium_findings.title: Secret Handling Requires User Caution
medium_findings.description: The skill includes API key and GitHub token placeholders in MCP environment blocks. These are not real credentials, but generated configurations could cause users to store sensitive tokens in local MCP configuration files without clear rotation or least-privilege guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly discusses environment variables and token placeholders. No real token or network exfiltration is present, so this is an operational safety concern rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub API URL Is Benign
low_findings.description: The static network finding points to the standard GitHub API endpoint inside an example configuration. No evidence found of a network request, data transmission, or credential exfiltration in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a service endpoint string in documentation, not executable code. The surrounding example does not send data anywhere.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings map to MCP description and heading text, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose about MCP integration and design. There is no cryptographic implementation in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Critical Combined-Risk Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer combined command, network, and credential-access indicators into a critical heuristic. Human review found these indicators are documentation examples and placeholders, with no executable code path or malicious instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combination exists only in example MCP configuration blocks. No actual code execution, secret collection, or outbound request behavior is implemented by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides MCP server templates that use npx commands and package arguments. Users should pin package versions and verify package provenance before enabling generated MCP servers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present as generated configuration patterns. They are not executed by the skill itself, but they are relevant to downstream user safety.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Secret Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses environment-variable examples for API keys and GitHub tokens. This is standard MCP practice, but generated output should avoid embedding raw secrets and should recommend scoped tokens.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is explicit, but it uses placeholders rather than actual secrets. The security impact depends on how users apply the examples.

content.value_statement: MCP setup can be hard to structure across tools, services, and authentication models. This skill helps users design clear server configurations, integration plans, and review checklists.
actual_capabilities: Drafts MCP server configuration structures for APIs, databases, and developer tools.
Explains authentication patterns using environment variables and scoped credentials.
Reviews MCP configurations for performance, validation, and error-handling concerns.
Suggests integration patterns for REST, GraphQL, cloud, and CI/CD services.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for MCP server setup and service connectivity.
limitations: Does not include executable source code for an MCP server.
Does not verify whether referenced npm packages are trustworthy or maintained.
Does not test generated configurations against a live MCP client.
Requires users to provide real service details, credentials, and deployment constraints.

use_cases.title: Create a service connector
use_cases.description: Design an MCP configuration for a SaaS API with required environment variables and expected capabilities.

use_cases.title: Review an internal MCP setup
use_cases.description: Check an existing MCP configuration for unsafe token handling, unclear package choices, and missing validation.

use_cases.title: Plan developer-tool integrations
use_cases.description: Map MCP server responsibilities for code analysis, testing, build systems, and CI/CD workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a basic MCP server
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a beginner-friendly MCP integration plan for a service named [service]. Include required permissions, environment variables, and setup checks.

prompt_templates.title: Configure an API connector
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an MCP server configuration for [API name]. Use scoped authentication, avoid raw secrets, and explain each configuration choice.

prompt_templates.title: Review an existing configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this MCP configuration for security, reliability, and performance issues. Highlight unsafe commands, token risks, and missing validation.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a production integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this MCP integration for production use. Cover package pinning, retries, connection pooling, observability, rate limits, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Create an MCP integration plan for GitHub issue management.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with required GitHub scopes, environment variable names, server responsibilities, installation cautions, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my MCP database connector configuration.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering credential placement, connection pooling, query limits, input validation, and operational monitoring.

output_examples.input: Optimize an MCP server used by a CI pipeline.
output_examples.output: A production checklist with package pinning, retry behavior, cache strategy, timeout settings, and failure-response guidance.

best_practices: Pin package versions and verify publishers before enabling generated MCP servers.
Use scoped environment variables and rotate credentials after testing.
Review generated configurations before running commands or granting service access.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys into prompts or shared configuration examples.
Do not run latest-version packages without checking package provenance.
Do not expose broad service tokens when a narrow permission scope is available.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create MCP integration plans, configuration structures, authentication guidance, and review checklists.

faq.question: Does it install MCP servers automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users must review and run any commands themselves.

faq.question: Can it handle GitHub MCP integrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes GitHub as an example and can guide token scopes, API endpoints, and setup checks.

faq.question: Is it safe to paste real tokens into prompts?
faq.answer: No. Use placeholder values in prompts and add real credentials only in your local secret-management process.

faq.question: Can it improve performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest connection pooling, retries, caching, rate-limit handling, and timeout settings.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers, platform engineers, and security reviewers who work with MCP server configurations.
